text.title: gitlab-boards Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for gitlab-boards as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using flat labels (no :: separator) for workflow states.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Flat labels allow multiple conflicting workflow labels simultaneously; the board becomes unreliable as a state representation.

failure-mode.description: Agent applies two workflow:: labels; GitLab keeps the last one silently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Scoped-label mutual exclusivity is enforced server-side by keeping the last label applied; the agent believes both stuck. Always verify with a read-after-write API call.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates an MR body with "Closes #999" without verifying the issue exists.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Auto-close hallucination: the referenced issue may not exist or may be in a different project; validate IDs before committing.

failure-mode.description: Agent uses REST PUT /issues to trigger Quick Actions like /spend or /assign.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Quick Actions only execute when submitted through the notes API or the issue body on creation/update — not via field updates in REST PUT.

failure-mode.description: Notification spam: bots comment on every state change.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Thread updates drown teams; prefer updating the existing thread rather than posting new comments per transition.
